    "Fall protection webbing is frayed and/or burned, indicating damaged load-bearing material.",
    "Damaged webbing cannot be assumed to meet the required strength and is unsafe for fall arrest use.",

  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the damaged fall protection equipment from service.",
    "Replace the frayed/burned webbing or the entire harness/lanyard/anchor assembly with compliant, inspected equipment.",
    "Inspect all other fall protection components on site for similar damage or defects.",
    "Document the removal and replacement, and obtain competent person sign-off before use."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140 require personal fall protection equipment to be inspected and maintained in safe condition. Frayed or burned webbing compromises the strength of the equipment and creates a life-safety hazard, making the installation defective and non-compliant. The work must fail until the damaged equipment is replaced.",

    "1910.140"
  ]
